content.problemStatement:
Consider an array BBB of length MMM.

The beauty of BBB is defined as ∑i=1M∣Bi−BM+1−i∣\sum_{i=1}^{M} |B_i-B_{M+1-i}|∑i=1M​∣Bi​−BM+1−i​∣.

The **score** of BBB is the **maximum** beauty over **all** permutations of BBB.

You are given positive integers NNN and XXX.

Find the sum of **scores** of all arrays AAA of length NNN, such that 1≤Ai≤X1 \leq A_i \leq X1≤Ai​≤X.

Since the sum might be large, please print it modulo 998244353998244353998244353.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains two space-separated integers NNN and XXX — the number of elements in AAA and the maximum allowed value of AiA_iAi​, respectively.




Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line, the required sum, modulo 998244353998244353998244353.
constraints: The sum of NNN over all testcases don't exceed 500050005000.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: The arrays are:

[1,1][1, 1][1,1]: Score of this array is ∣1−1∣+∣1−1∣=0|1-1| + |1-1|= 0∣1−1∣+∣1−1∣=0.
[1,2][1, 2][1,2]: Score of this array is 222 since rearrangement [1,2][1, 2][1,2] has maximum beauty.
[1,3][1, 3][1,3]: Score of this array is 444 since rearrangement [1,3][1, 3][1,3] has maximum beauty.
[2,1][2, 1][2,1]: Score of this array is 222 since rearrangement [1,2][1, 2][1,2] has maximum beauty.
[2,2][2, 2][2,2]: Score of this array is 000.
[2,3][2, 3][2,3]: Score of this array is 222 since rearrangement [2,3][2, 3][2,3] has maximum beauty.
[3,1][3, 1][3,1]: Score of this array is 444 since rearrangement [1,3][1, 3][1,3] has maximum beauty.
[3,2][3, 2][3,2]: Score of this array is 222 since rearrangement [2,3][2, 3][2,3] has maximum beauty.
[3,3][3, 3][3,3]: Score of this array is 000.

The sum of scores is 2+4+2+2+4+2=162+4+2+2+4+2 = 162+4+2+2+4+2=16.
